$comment: Creature-state thresholds on the SMOOTHED burn rate (tokens/min, input+output only; cache excluded). Calibrated against real Claude Code sessions: idle=0, light work ~1-7k/min, heavy bursts ~28k/min. Each state has separate up/down cutoffs (hysteresis) so a noisy signal does not strobe. Tune freely — read live in dev, no rebuild needed.

sleeping.$note: entered when no active block; not rate-driven

onfire.$note: onfire requires the high rate to be sustained, not a single spike

spent.$note: spent = the crash AFTER burning hot: when the rate collapses below rateThreshold within afterOnfireSeconds of being onfire, the rat is spent for durationSeconds (then relaxes to calm). NOT tied to quota exhaustion.

spike.$note: instant-rate jump (tokens/min) above this triggers a transient flinch bounce

$activityFloorNote: Fast-attack perk-up: if a token was written within this many seconds, the rat is at least 'working' immediately (don't wait for the smoothed rate to climb). Higher states still come from the smoothed rate.
$idleTimeoutNote: Smart napping: the nap clock runs from the last conversational line (user or assistant), so a new message resets it; 'waiting' (an open question) never naps. This is the hold time (seconds) after the last activity before the rat sleeps.
$doneHoldNote: How long to hold the 'done' pose after a finished turn before the rat naps. 120 = 2 minutes.
$sentHoldNote: After the user sends a message we're awaiting Claude. Hold the idle pose this long before napping — longer than idleTimeoutSeconds so the rat doesn't nap through the 'dead air' before Claude starts responding. 180 = 3 minutes.
